ideal_must_detect.matched_text: All external communications require joint approval
none of which has been publicly announced

Structure: Buyer to acquire a 35% fully diluted stake via (i) subscription to Series B preferred shares and (ii) secondary purchase from select founders, with a Php 150,000,000 bridge convertible note available at Buyer’s option.
Consideration: Aggregate purchase price of Php 1,800,000,000, of which 10% to be placed in escrow for 12 months; customary working-capital and net-debt adjustments at closing.

Blackout and Trading Restrictions: Directors, officers, and employees of the parties with access to material non-public information are subject to a trading blackout effective upon circulation of this term sheet until public disclosure, consistent with the SRC and SEC guidance on tipping and insider trading.

Information Barriers: Due diligence to be conducted through a Clean Team subject to information-barrier protocols; access to cyber logs, wallet custody procedures, and breach reports will be ring-fenced in a secure VDR with named-user access only.

Cyber Incident CP: The Company has disclosed an internal 14 April 2026 security incident involving unauthorized access to a KYC database (approx. 9,842 customer emails) managed by SierraCloud Technologies Corp.; closing is conditioned on timely NPC notification, remediation, and independent verification, none of which has been publicly announced.

Crypto/DPT and Custody: The Company’s affiliate Baybay Digital Assets OPC has a pending BSP VASP application; closing requires either issuance of the VASP authority or implementation of an approved third-party custodial arrangement, with no public communications until formal announcements are cleared by Compliance.

ESG Earnout: An additional Php 150,000,000 earnout is payable upon verification of 180,000 tCO2e emissions avoided within 24 months post-closing, certified by Philippine Sustainability Verifiers, Inc., with data subject to third-party assurance.

must_detect.matched_text:
yer) and Mabuhay Verde Renewables, Inc. (Company), with contacts Atty. Liza F. Mercado (General Counsel, liza.mercado@isla-cap.example) and Mr. Ramon C. Licayan (CFO, ramon.licayan@mabuhayverde.example).
Structure: Buyer to acquire a 35% fully diluted stake via (i) subscription to Series B preferred shares and (ii) secondary purchase from select founders, with a Php 150,000,000 bridge convertible note available at Buyer’s option.
Consideration: Aggregate purchase price of Php 1,800,000,000, of which 10% to be placed in escrow for 12 months; customary working-capital and net-debt adjustments at closing.
Use of Proceeds: Expansion of 50MW rooftop solar program and ESG/climate compliance projects (including a non-public internal sandbox for tokenized renewable energy credits), subject to agreed milestones.
Regulatory and Disclosure: Transactions subject to Philippine SEC approvals/confirmations (as applicable) and disclosure on PSE EDGE within 24 hours of signing if any PSE-listed affiliate of the parties incurs a disclosure trigger under the SRC and PSE rules.
Exclusivity and No-Shop: Company grants Buyer a 60-day exclusivity period from countersignature; Company shall not solicit or entertain competing proposals during this period.
Blackout and Trading Restrictions: Directors, officers, and employees of the parties with access to material non-public information are subject to a trading blackout effective upon circulation of this term sheet until public disclosure, consistent with the SRC and SEC guidance on tipping and insider trading.
Insider List: Each party shall maintain and update an insider list for the transaction; initial access persons include Atty. Liza F. Mercado and Mr. Ramon C. Licayan, to be lodged with the Buyer’s Compliance Officer and retained for five years.
Information Barriers: Due diligence to be conducted through a Clean Team subject to information-barrier protocols; access to cyber logs, wallet custody procedures, and breach reports will be ring-fenced in a secure VDR with named-user access only.
Cyber Incident CP: The Company has disclosed an internal 14 April 2026 security incident involving unauthorized access to a KYC database (approx. 9,842 customer emails) managed by SierraCloud Technologies Corp.; closing is conditioned on timely NPC notification, remediation, and independent verification, none of which has been publicly announced.
Crypto/DPT and Custody: The Company’s affiliate Baybay Digital Assets OPC has a pending BSP VASP application; closing requires either issuance of the VASP authority or implementation of an approved third-party custodial arrangement, with no public communications until formal announcements are cleared by Compliance.
ESG Earnout: An additional Php 150,000,000 earnout is payable upon verification of 180,000 tCO2e emissions avoided within 24 months post-closing, certified by Philippine Sustainability Verifiers, Inc., with data subject to third-party assurance.
Selective Disclosure: All external communications require joint approval; no selective disclosure to analysts, customers, or vendors is permitted, and inquiries must be routed to liza.mercado@isla-cap.example—tipping is strictly prohibited.
Taxes: Parties shall bear their respective taxes; documentary stamp tax on share issuance shall be for the Company’s account, while any capital gains tax on the secondary sale shall be for the selling shareholders; stock transaction tax is inapplicable to this off-exchange transfer; appropriate filings with the BIR will be coordinated.
Educational/Operations-Only Note: The Company’s internal training module references “trading blackout,” “crypto wallet,” and a “breach playbook” purely as examples for staff orientation and does not contain any deal-stage or market-sensitive information.
Governing Law and Disputes: This term sheet is governed by the laws of the Republic of the Philippines, venue in Makati City; disputes to be settled by arbitration under PDRCI rules,
